intro: Pneumonanthe forwoodii, also known as Forwood's lungwort, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae, native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pneumonanthe forwoodii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, as well as extracts in capsule or tincture form, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions.
